title: SOAP Envelope Structure
description: JSON Structure documentation for a SOAP 1.2 message envelope as defined by the W3C SOAP 1.2 specification

fields.name: encodingStyle
fields.description: A URI identifying the serialization rules used to encode the SOAP message parts. May appear on any element in the SOAP message.

fields.name: Header
fields.description: Optional container for metadata and processing instructions. Contains zero or more header blocks targeting specific SOAP nodes.

fields.name: header_blocks
fields.description: Header entries (header blocks) targeting specific SOAP nodes

attributes.mustUnderstand: boolean — if true, targeted node MUST process or fault
attributes.role: uri — identifies the SOAP node role this block targets
attributes.relay: boolean — if true, relay this block if not processed

fields.name: Body
fields.description: Required container for the main payload of the SOAP message, targeting the ultimate receiver.

fields.name: payload
fields.description: The actual message content — typically the operation request/response or a Fault element

fields.name: Fault
fields.description: Error information returned when message processing fails

fields.name: Reason
fields.description: Human-readable explanation of the fault, with language attributes

fields.name: Node
fields.description: URI identifying the SOAP node that generated the fault

fields.name: Role
fields.description: URI identifying the role the faulting node was operating in
